page: Microsoft Azure E96-24as_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Easv7 family. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 12 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. This server is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU tests, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all scoring in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-threaded performance is more moderate, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and PassMark CPU scores falling into the average tier (middle 50%). Memory performance is robust, showing a strong PassMark memory score (top 25%), while large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths remain in the average tier. For lightweight AI workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 435.76 tokens per second on a small 135M model, though performance scales down to average levels for medium 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E96-24as_v7 offers high memory density relative to its compute core count, making it a specialized option for memory-bound workloads rather than compute-heavy parallel processing. The lack of local storage means users must rely on external network-attached storage, which introduces an architectural dependency. Given the high RAM capacity and strong single-core performance, this server is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, caching layers like Redis, and lightweight language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E96-24as_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the Easv7 family, featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs and 768.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz, it delivers a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. The server lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong memory performance and fast text generation for small language models. However, multi-core CPU benchmarks and database operations sit in the average tier. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads, caching systems, and database operations that require substantial memory capacity but do not demand extreme multi-core parallel processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96-24as_v7 is a memory-optimized server featuring 24 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 768 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, suited for memory-intensive database workloads.
